purpose: Post-SME-audit confound-controlled reanalysis of family clustering and alignment-regime claims

likelihood_ratio_test_regime_adds_info_b.interpretation: regime does NOT add info beyond family — fully confounded in this manifest

family_only_singleton_collapsed.description: Family ANOVA with 4 singleton cells (Cohere, DeepSeek, Mistral, Gemma) collapsed to "Other"

manuscript_implication.regime_claim: RETRACT. The five-bucket regime taxonomy is structurally collinear with provider for 4 of 6 cells (CAI=4/4 Anthropic, RLHF=5/5 OpenAI, Hybrid=4/4 Google, RLHF-RAG=1/1 Cohere). LRT p=1.000: regime adds zero information beyond family. Drop the "alignment-regime-dependent" claim from abstract, §1, §5, §7. Move the 5-bucket taxonomy to §6 limitations as known design flaw of the cross-rater atlas.
manuscript_implication.family_claim: STRENGTHEN. Family clustering survives singleton-cell collapse (η² = 0.743, p = 0.0002, vs original 0.755, p = 0.003 with singleton inflation). Bootstrap 95% CI [0.539, 0.960]. Report ICC permutation p = 0.003 as primary; report η² with CI; acknowledge singleton inflation in original η².
manuscript_implication.descriptive_finding: Google is the family at lowest |r| (mean 0.587, n=4). Report this descriptively without regime framing. The mechanism question (why Google?) is now framed as descriptive in this paper, with the alignment-regime hypothesis queued for atlas v2 with 2+ providers per regime cell.
